sections.paragraph:
i.q. נָפַח (which see), to breathe, to blow. (Arab. فَاحَ and فاخ to exhale odour, to be fragrant.) Son 2:17, עַד שֶׁיָּפוּחַ הַיֹּום “until the day breathe”, i.e. until the heat departs, until evening; Son 4:6. Compare רוּחַ.

Hiphil-

(1) followed by an acc. to blow through, Son 4:16.

(2) followed by בְּ to blow (a fire) Ezek. 21:36 followed by an acc. Pro 29:8, “blow upon a city,” i.e. excite sedition.

(3) figuratively, to blow out, to utter, as lies, Pro 6:19 14:5 19:5 , Pro 19:9in a good sense, to utter (the truth), 12:17 .

(4) to pant, to hasten, Hab 2:3. Compare שָׁאַף Ecc 1:5.

(5) to rail against any one, followed by בְּ Psa 10:5 and לְ 12:6 , אֲשֶׁר יָפִיחַ לֹו “whom they reproached”, i.e. the oppressed.

Derivative, פִּיחַ.
